title: Passing Parameters
description: Give your functions the information they need.

slides.title: Providing Context 📦
slides.content:
## Flexible Recipes

Our `greetUser()` function always says the exact same thing. But what if we want to greet a specific person by their specific name?

We need to pass data INTO the function using **Parameters**.

slides.title: Adding Parameters 📥
slides.content:
Inside the empty parentheses `()`, we define a variable name and its data type, separated by a colon `:`.

Now, anyone calling this function MUST provide a String to fill that variable.

quizData.question: If you define `fun greet(name: String)`, what happens if you call `greet(25)`?

options.text: It creates a crash/compile error because 25 is an Int, not a String.
It converts 25 to a String automatically.

quizData.explanation: Kotlin is strictly typed. The function explicitly demanded a String, so you cannot give it an Int.

slides.title: Multiple Parameters ➕➕
slides.content: You can request as many pieces of information as you want. Just separate them with a comma `,`.

quizData.question: True or False: The order in which you pass values must exactly match the order the parameters were defined.
quizData.explanation: If the definition says `(String, Int)`, you must pass them exactly as `"Text", 50`. You cannot pass `50, "Text"`.
